title: FILE_SIZE_BYTES for Parquet doesn't work properly in parallel
description:
### What happens?

When running a command like `COPY 'big_parquet.parquet' TO 'output.parquet' (FILE_SIZE_BYTES '16MB')` duckdb doesn't properly split the output into the target size, instead the output will look like the following:

```
-rw-rw-r-- 1 user user  37M Oct 30 10:46 data_1.parquet
-rw-rw-r-- 1 user user 813M Oct 30 10:46 data_0.parquet
-rw-rw-r-- 1 user user  347 Oct 30 10:46 data_3.parquet
-rw-rw-r-- 1 user user  26M Oct 30 10:46 data_2.parquet
```

Note that there is one 813MB file (data_0) way over the limit size And another file which contains no actual content (data_3)

I've narrowed this issue's cause to between 1.2.2 and 1.3.0 I think it is likely related to these changes: https://github.com/duckdb/duckdb/pull/16928

This causes annoying metadata overhead, particularly annoying when the data is remote with the 0 row file, but at the same time it doesn't actually split the files, which prevents other optimizations

Highly related to: https://github.com/duckdb/ducklake/issues/525

### To Reproduce

1. Dataset generation:
```sql
COPY (
      WITH n AS (
          SELECT 4230875 AS nrows
      )
      SELECT
          abs(random() * 1000000000000)::UBIGINT AS col1,
          TIMESTAMP '2020-01-01' + INTERVAL (random() * 3650) DAY AS col2,
          (random() * 100000)::UINTEGER AS col3,
          TIMESTAMP '2020-01-01' + INTERVAL (random() * 3650) DAY AS col4,
          md5(random()::VARCHAR) AS col5,
          md5(random()::VARCHAR) AS col6,
          random() AS col7,
          random() AS col8,
          random() AS col9,
          random() AS col10,
          random() AS col11,
          random() AS col12,
          random() AS col13,
          random() AS col14,
          random() AS col15,
          random() AS col16,
          random() AS col17,
          random() AS col18,
          random() AS col19,
          md5(random()::VARCHAR) AS col20
      FROM range((SELECT nrows FROM n))
  )
  TO 'combined_synthetic.parquet' (FORMAT PARQUET);
```
Note that this issue only occurs when copying from a parquet file, if we were to put the file size bytes restriction on generating 'combined_synthetic.parquet' it properly splits, there is a relationship with parallism, explicitly setting the number of threads to 1 eliminates the issue, and leads to much more uniform file sizes and no empty files

2. Copy the data
```sql
COPY 'combined_synthetic.parquet' TO 'output.parquet' (FILE_SIZE_BYTES '16MB');
```
3. Inspect the output directory

### OS:

Linux x86_64

### DuckDB Version:

1.3.0 through 1.5.0

### DuckDB Client:

CLI

### Hardware:

_No response_

### Full Name:

Julian Meyers

### Affiliation:

bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE test_data (id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY, value VARCHAR, created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP);
CREATE TABLE more_data AS SELECT id, value, created_at FROM test_data WHERE value IS NOT NULL;
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM more_data WHERE created_at < TIMESTAMP '2021-01-01';
key_bug_features: FILE_SIZE_BYTES not splitting output files in parallel
